title: cycnium ajugifolium
intro: Cycnium ajugifolium, also known as the 'blue toadflax', is a perennial plant in the borage family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and dysentery. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, with its health benefits attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. Cycnium ajugifolium is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the dried leaves and stems used in herbal preparations to treat a range of ailments.
